title: hibbertia quadricolor var. benthamiana
intro: Hibbertia quadricolor var. benthamiana, also known as the yellow daisy or Guinea flower, is a flowering plant species native to Australia. It is known for its bright yellow flowers and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, such as soothing skin irritations and reducing fever, and is often used to treat colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Hibbertia quadricolor var. benthamiana include teas and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
